issue_title: updating the local variables may hang gdb
issue_body:
In the program I'm trying to debug there's a local variable which is a class instance with a static member of the same type. This causes gdb to enter an endless loop whenever it is displayed (e.g. with `info locals` or when generating the response to `-stack-list-variables --simple-values`), and this causes gdbgui to hang in turn until I send `SIGINT` to gdb.

I have two suggestions:

 + a workaround: make gdbgui only issue the `stack-list-variables` query when the locals pane is not collapsed. This way I'll still be able to step through the code by hiding the locals.
 
 + a possible fix: use `-stack-list-variables --no-values` to query the locals and function args and then create a var for each (i.e. start "watching" all locals) - this seems to work when I do it manually...  (mmm, you'd probably need to remove all the watched locals and re-add them every time the gui is updated)

issue_title: How do I terminate Pools cleanly?
issue_body:
Looking at #70, this looks like a difficult problem. I would like a way to shut down cleanly a Kernel using pools when the user cancels the program (e.g. `KeyboardInterrupt`ing it). The most obvious problem is in `curio/workers.py`, here https://github.com/dabeaz/curio/blob/master/curio/workers.py#L284: By the time the finally is executed, `shutdown` is already called, setting the workers to `None`, and thus resulting in an attribute error. Once that is worked around, with e.g.
````diff
diff --git a/curio/workers.py b/curio/workers.py
index 78895fd..d779d99 100644
--- a/curio/workers.py
+++ b/curio/workers.py
@@ -282,7 +282,8 @@ class WorkerPool(object):
                 worker = self.workercls()
                 raise
             finally:
-                self.workers.append(worker)
+                if self.workers is not None:
+                    self.workers.append(worker)
````

There are still some issues. If I have a simple program like:
````python
import curio

def f():
    import time
    time.sleep(40)

async def main():
    coro = curio.run_in_process(f)
    task = await curio.spawn(coro)

kernel = curio.Kernel()
try:
    kernel.run(main())
except KeyboardInterrupt:
    print("OK")
````

and I run it and then press Ctrl+C, it almost works, except for some noisy multiprocessing delete action:

````
$ python  simple.py 
^COK
Process Process-1:
````

If I change `f()` to explicitly ignore the interrupt signal, it shuts quiet:

````python
def f():
    import time
    import signal
    signal.signal(signal.SIGINT, signal.SIG_IGN)
    time.sleep(40)
````

````sh
$ python  simple.py 
^COK
````

However, if I have a more complex code (specifically this https://github.com/NNPDF/reportengine/blob/25f7b0c4680a91b692653e24b1a0b5f8c222dc99/src/reportengine/resourcebuilder.py#L135) with more tasks being ran and waited for I get the dreaded `GeneratorExit`thing 4 times, once for each concurrent run_in_process. Not really sure why it behaves differently from the simple case:

````
Exception ignored in: <bound method Task.__del__ of Task(id=9, <coroutine object run_in_process at 0x7f6dadf60830>, state='READ_WAIT')>

issue_title: MetaPacket changes break pcap's LE classes
issue_body:
_From [jon.ober...@gmail.com](https://code.google.com/u/109518670617871611778/) on July 28, 2007 02:47:02_

The MetaPacket changes in r39 break LEPktHdr and LEFileHdr of pcap.py (and
any other similar classes).  Since the clsdict only contains the
__byte_order__ endianess modifier and inherits the __hdr__ from the base
class, the __hdr_fmt__ won't be set in MetaPacket's __new__.

Patch attached to add a testcase to pcap.py that exercises the issue.

**Attachment:** [dpkt-pcap-endian-test.patch](http://code.google.com/p/dpkt/issues/detail?id=6)

_Original issue: http://code.google.com/p/dpkt/issues/detail?id=6_

The output of `thefuck --version` (something like `The Fuck 3.1 using Python 3.5.0`):

The Fuck 3.27 using Python 3.7.0

Your shell and its version (`bash`, `zsh`, *Windows PowerShell*, etc.):

PowerShell Core 6.0.2

Your system (Debian 7, ArchLinux, Windows, etc.):

Windows 10.0.7134 (April Creator's Update)

How to reproduce the bug:

Attempt to invoke thefuck while using PowerShell Core (aka `pwsh`)

The output of The Fuck with `THEFUCK_DEBUG=true` exported (typically execute `export THEFUCK_DEBUG=true` in your shell before The Fuck):

Skipping this one. Will explain why below.

If the bug only appears with a specific application, the output of that application and its version:

N/A

Anything else you think is relevant:

So the issue is actually in thefuck's main `[__init.py__](https://github.com/nvbn/thefuck/blob/master/thefuck/shells/__init__.py)`. The mapping between shell process name and shell type is incomplete. PowerShell Core's process name is `pwsh`. This doesn't exist, so when running `pwsh`, thefuck just uses __init__'s `Generic()`, which seems to treat the environment as a bash-alike.

I'm pretty sure the fix is simple: change the mapping to look like this:

```python
shells = {'bash': Bash,
          'fish': Fish,
          'zsh': Zsh,
          'csh': Tcsh,
          'tcsh': Tcsh,
          'powershell': Powershell,
          'pwsh': Powershell}
``` 

I can open a PR for this, but I don't think I have the bandwidth to fully set up the environment or do testing on this, so I'd rather leave this breadcrumb for someone else to pick up on.

Hello, 

Since version 0.4.1, legacy `None` initializer support has been removed in pyasn1 https://github.com/etingof/pyasn1/pull/91

This introduces the following issue:

```
[...]
search_filter = '(&(objectCategory=person)(!(userAccountControl:1.2.840.113556.1.4.803:=2))(distinguishedName=' + dn + '))'
if conn.search(search_base=host_dn, search_filter=search_filter, search_scope=ldap3.SUBTREE, attributes=fields.keys()):
[...]
```

```userAccountControl:1.2.840.113556.1.4.803:=2``` causes `dn_attributes` to retain the [default value of `None`](https://github.com/cannatag/ldap3/blob/master/ldap3/operation/search.py#L111) for the matching filter [initialization](https://github.com/cannatag/ldap3/blob/master/ldap3/operation/search.py#L267)

```
[...]pyasn1\type\univ.py", line 254, in prettyIn
    return int(value)
TypeError: int() argument must be a string, a bytes-like object or a number, not 'NoneType'
```

Either the requirements should be changed to ```pyasn1>=0.1.8,<0.4.1``` or at least the default value for `dn_attributes` should be changed to `False`.

issue_title: New CryptedFileKeyring doesn't follow keyring protocol
issue_body:
If I understand correctly (and please correct me if not), the new CryptedFileKeyring does not follow the keyring protocol, but instead attempts to implement a similar protocol. Rather than implementing the abstract methods of the BasicFileKeyring, it encrypts and decrypts the entire file, bypassing the machinery implemented in the parent class.

At the very least, this causes the tests to fail (when attempting to call encrypt and decrypt).

It may be the case that this implementation is sound as simply needs to not subclass from BasicFileKeyring and have the tests updated accordingly.

----------------------------------------
- Bitbucket: https://bitbucket.org/kang/python-keyring-lib/issue/64
- Originally reported by: Jason R. Coombs
- Originally created at: 2012-06-09T20:30:11.103

issue_body:

----

I am writing to you concerning some troubleshooting related to the keyring module.

I have some python code which queries an encrypted user name and password previously set up with the keyring tools.
This is the call I have in the code:

1 keyring.get_keyring().keychain = '/path_to/login.keychain'
2 password = keyring.get_password(“server", “user”)

This call works perfectly fine when I execute my code interactively, say :

python mycode.py

getting the requested password and continuing the code execution.

However, I aim to run this code via the crontab environment (i.e. in the backend).
When doing so,I get this output:

In addition to precision, recall, and f-measure, MIREX also computes the Average Overlap Ratio (defined in [this paper](http://ismir2009.ismir.net/proceedings/PS2-21.pdf)). Furthermore, it also computes all four metrics (p, r, f1, o) but with mapping the pitch values to chroma values (i.e. so that octave errors aren't penalized).

This means that MIREX computes 16 metrics in total:
(Precision, Recall, F-measure, AverageOR) x (with offsets, without offsets ) x (pitch, chroma) = 16

Currently we have:
(Precision, Recall, F-measure) x (with offsets, without offsets ) = 6

issue_title: fake_libc_include not included in distributed version of library
issue_body:
The `fake_libc_include` directory is not included in the version of the library installed from PyPI with pip.

This means that I cannot do something like:

```
pycparser_path = os.path.basename(pycparser.__file__)
include_path = os.path.join(pycparser_path, 'utils', 'fake_libc_include')
ast = parse_file(filename, use_cpp=True, cpp_args=rf'-I{include_path}')
```

Instead I have to vendor `fake_libc_include` (which is not the end of the world!).

Would you consider a patch to fix this?

Problem is here:
https://github.com/giampaolo/psutil/blob/08964b08700fcfc5a4917a01107009a3ee913341/psutil/__init__.py#L473
This is a race condition as the process may be alive at `ppid = self.ppid()` but not at `self.create_time()`. It's a very small time frame but the issues technically exists.

issue_title: Any URIs with unicode characters in them throw an exception in middleware
issue_body:
Whenever a URI is processed through the **whitenoise** middleware, if there are any unicode characters in that URI, an exception is thrown and results in a 500 error if being run in a Django server. This **only occurs when the *autorefresh* setting is *True***. It seemingly only affects development environments.

For example:

```bash
$ curl -X GET http://127.0.0.1:8000/static/
```

will result in a stack trace like so, which is an uncaught exception in Django middleware, causing a 500.

```python
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File ".../lib/python2.7/wsgiref/handlers.py", line 85, in run
    self.result = application(self.environ, self.start_response)
  File ".../lib/python2.7/site-packages/whitenoise/base.py", line 57, in __call__
    static_file = self.find_file(environ['PATH_INFO'])
  File ".../lib/python2.7/site-packages/whitenoise/django.py", line 75, in find_file
    if self.use_finders and url.startswith(self.static_prefix):
UnicodeDecodeError: 'ascii' codec can't decode byte 0xe2 in position 78: ordinal not in range(128)
```

This can be reproduced on a bare Django project (I am using v1.9.6, Python 2.7.11) simply by configuring the STATIC_URL and adding the whitenoise middleware.

Looking in the library, it seems like when the **PATH_INFO** environment variable is pulled (whitenoise/base.py:57), it is left as a plain byte string instead of being decoded into a unicode object. Decoding this str object at this spot seems to fix my issue, but I don't know if other places need this as well.

It seems the crux of this error can be reproduced by the following code:
```python
>>> b""
'\xef\xa3\xbf'
>>> b"".startswith(u"some text")
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
UnicodeDecodeError: 'ascii' codec can't decode byte 0xef in position 0: ordinal not in range(128)
```

issue_title: Large coordinates are not returned exactly, because of conversion to floats
issue_body:
I've noticed that the coordinates of the paths returned by `pyclipper.Execute()` are represented using floats. This introduces some inaccuracies for very large coordinate values as not all values of an `int64_t` can be represented by a `double`. I've added a test case here showing the problem:

https://github.com/Feuermurmel/pyclipper/tree/inexact-coordinates

I see that the library supports automatic scaling of coordinate values by setting the global `pyclipper.SCALING_FACTOR`. This of course means that, unless this variable is set to 1, a type other than `int` is necessary to represent coordinates. Thus I'm not sure what the best way is to solve this problem. I currently see the following options:

– Remove `pyclipper.SCALING_FACTOR`, but there are probably some people using it.
– Only use floats when `pyclipper.SCALING_FACTOR` is not set to 1. Seems a bit hack-ish.
– Add a second implementation of `pyclipper.Pyclipper` which ignores the scaling factor and uses ints instead. This seems obscure and bloats the library.

The `analysis.py` module has this line:
```
        # a little error checking
        if fn: assert fn.im_self is self
```
Which should (1) be removed or (2) figure out what it was protecting against and documenting it, it doesn't work in Python3.  It also should be updated to PEP8 naming conventions, which will prompt a set of coordinating changes in the [bacpypes-pcap](https://github.com/JoelBender/bacpypes-pcap) project.

issue_title: submit should nag if submission file is not a zip (because upload BW is so low)
issue_body:
submit should warn if submission file is not a zip. This can make the difference between 10min upload time vs 30sec. Due to the [low upload bandwidth (< 1Mbps)](https://github.com/floydwch/kaggle-cli/issues/16).

(Also this warning should be suppressable, for users who really insist to do that.)

Also, as to bandwidth, it would be nice to report file size, upload time and **BW (Mbps)** after upload. Then users could automatically figure out what times of day (UTC-8) are fast/slow.
Probably also useful to report which proxy, if any, was used, as that may affect things.

issue_title: Xpub header is Ltub for Litecoin but perhaps should be the same as BTC
issue_body:
I tried to use my xpub from Electrum to generate a watching only wallet, but it failed. After digging around I realized Electrum was using xpub, not Ltub. I found that strange, and thought it was a mistake in Electrum but it seems that it is actually pywallet that isn't following conventions, according to this LTC dev:
[Response from LTC dev](https://www.reddit.com/r/litecoin/comments/7rorqa/electrum_using_bitcoin_xpub_headers/dszq9d5/?context=3)

I am the developer of Construct (a parsing library) since I took over the project 2 years ago. Please refer any other projects that also use this library to this post, or copy it over to them. 

Construct is undergoing heavy changes at the moment, similar to those between 2.5->2.8, including addition of compiler feature (to make it much faster than Kaitai), but also several classes are going to be removed (for a good reason), and also few were added. API will be unstable for about a month.

What you can do:
- All scheduled work is posted as tickets, in [Issues](https://github.com/construct/construct/issues) and also on [Kanban](https://github.com/construct/construct/projects/3) page. If you subscribe (watch repository) then you will have advance notice of any incoming changes. Unfortunately those tickets also include minor work items, so you would get some clutter notifications too. I appologise for that.
- I encourage everyone to browse those tickets, vote for feature requests, but also post objections for planned removal of features, or changes in semantics. Subscribing therefore is recommended.
- You can find what was already changed (at any moment) on [Transition page](https://construct.readthedocs.io/en/latest/transition29.html), which is like a growing changelog. It gets updated whenever something gets added or removed, but since the order is not chronological, new items do not end up on top or bottom of the list.
- You can find currently available classes and semantics on revamped [Core API pages](https://construct.readthedocs.io/en/latest/index.html#api-reference). Those pages are very extensive and always uptodate with the implementation.
- **Feel free, at any point in time, to open a new Issue and ask questions or make complaints** about breakage. At minimum, I can offer advice and possible alternatives. People very often use the classes in ways that were not designed for, like use String classes to process Bytes.
- **Feel free, at any point in time, to open a new Issue and request changes.** If you need a new class, and make a good clear case, I would be more than willing to add it to core library to support your project.
- API should be stable within a month, so either do version pinning and deal with it then, or keep fixing your code at every release (which is ~3 day cycle at the moment).

Close this topic at your discretion.

issue_title: Remote API for DoS mitigation, stats, ...
issue_body:
When we have #126 (DoS mitigation setting) it would be nice for operations to be able to turn this on or off via an API call (rather than re-starting the server). Similarly, it would be nice to have an API to ask for statistics (instead of reading the "stats.json" every X minutes and hoping the server wrote a new one).

@exarkun and I were discussing how clients might authenticate to this API. The properties we want are:

- authenticate the client to the server (make sure attackers can't control DoS protection)
- authenticate the server to the client (make sure attackers can't trick control plane into thinking DoS protection is on when it's off)
- authenticate the message (make sure attackers can't change the request in flight to achieve their desired DoS protection state)
- don't want an attacker who can record a client->server request to be able to reply it later (e.g. to reset state)

From an Ops perspective it would be best to avoid dynamically-created persistent state.

We discussed using the following scheme:

- clients and servers each have a keypair
- all requests from clients to server are some JSON put inside a `nacl.Box` to the server's pubkey and sent via an HTTP POST to an URL starting with (e.g.) `http://localhost:4001/api/v1/`
- all replies from server to client are some JSON put inside a `nacl.Box` to the client's pubkey and sent as the HTTP response
- requests and replies should include a nonce or timestamp to prevent replays.

The server learns about its private key via a `--private-key-file` option, which it reads as the key data (`nacl.encoders.HexEncoder` -encoded. The server learns about authorized client keys via a `--authorized-api-clients` option which is a file containing client public keys, one per line. Each line shall be `"client_nick hex_encoded_key\n"`. Both of these files are ASCII only.

The sever also needs an additional option to tell it where to listen for the API (e.g. `--api-endpoint tcp:4001:interface=localhost` if using Twisted endpoint-strings).

We could write a `wormhole-api` command-line client. It will learn its private key the same way as the server (via a `--private-key-file` option) and will learn the server's public key via a command-line option (e.g. `--server-pubkey <hex-encoded-data>`)

When I tried to install magic-wormhole on Ubuntu 16.04 (Xenial Xerus), it appeared to work fine, but I know when I ran apt-cache policy magic-wormhole, nothing showed as being installed;
When I ran sudo apt-get install magic-wormhole, I got back this:
...
E: Unable to locate package magic-wormhole
So I am missing something, or not doing something right. Sorry to sound like a newbie/doofus, but really, normally, I don't have these issues.
Anyone help me? Thanks (no trolling me, please, for my ignorance; thanks).

I wanted to add support for the `compilescss` command because currently the command does not support jinja2. There have been a lot of changes in django-compressor's compress command which added support for jinja2 since the compilescss here was written based on that one. I think we/me should refactor the command and inherit from the compress command. This way we can follow the changes there. The current command on django-compressor is very messy, I think it's best to refactor that on their end first and then inherit it here, but add some changes so instead of compressing it would compile SCSS.

It might also be good to add to the readme that setting `SASS_PROCESSOR_ENABLED` is the only way to use Jinja2 with `DEBUG` set to `False`.

issue_title: Unable to get django-rest-framework-bulk to update instances
issue_body:
I've tried creating a very simple API endpoint following the README:

```
from django.db import models

from rest_framework import serializers
from rest_framework_bulk import BulkListSerializer, BulkSerializerMixin, ListBulkCreateUpdateAPIView


class Foo(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=20)
    class Meta:
        app_label = "foobar"


class FooSerializer(BulkSerializerMixin, serializers.ModelSerializer):
    class Meta(object):
        model = Foo
        list_serializer_class = BulkListSerializer

class FooView(ListBulkCreateUpdateAPIView):
    queryset = Foo.objects.all()
    serializer_class = FooSerializer
```


I've configured a URL for `FooView` and am able to load the built in `rest_framework` html page for this API endpoint. I've also created a few instances of the `Foo` model for testing. 

However, when I attempt to make an HTTP PUT/PATCH request to this endpoint to update one of the existing instances, I get a successful return code but no update actually occurs. 

`curl 'http://127.0.0.1:8000/foo/' -X PUT -H 'Cookie: csrftoken=M8jkbJtUKwjKB7zya8JYvxqJVxkRaPgG'  -H 'X-CSRFToken: M8jkbJtUKwjKB7zya8JYvxqJVxkRaPgG' --data 'id=1&name=D' --compressed`

The response is simply an empty list `[]` and the instance with id=1 retains its old name. 

[I've created a sample project to reproduce this](https://github.com/JDougherty/drftest), and would really appreciate any help. I am unable to determine if this is a bug in DRF, DRF-bulk, or a misunderstanding on my part.
At pypi, the license is listed as MIT, but there's nothing in the source to indicate that.

I've been using Daphne in production for a few months now, and it works really great. But lately as we were scaling I started having some troubles with number of connections. 

Stack: I run `nginx` as a reverse proxy that routes all `WebSocket` traffic to Daphne over HTTP. I have defined `upstream daphne` with multiple entries for each of Daphne instances (which are running on different ports, bound to `0.0.0.0`). 

I've seen that clients have trouble connecting WebSocket when Daphne hits ca. 250 open file descriptors (I guess 1 `fd` per connection, so that would suggest 250 connections). System-wide file descriptor limit is not a problem, as it is very high and certainly not hit.

From what I've seen in Channels benchmarks I would suppose Daphne could handle many more connections (especially if it was handling HTTP requests as well). Am I missing something? Maybe we could work on some example configurations and/or tips&tricks for scaling so other people can have easier times doing so?

Hi,

I am currently using nd.contrib.fft to compute STFT(short time Fourier transform) of audio wavs to implement the distilled WaveNet. 
I noticed that even I set the weight of STFT loss to 0 (0 * STFT_loss), there are still gradients backward from STFT_loss to parameters, and this behavior causes the gradient to be NaN if the weight is not 0 (1 * STFT_loss)
I have triple checked my implemented and this is the only possible place that may raise an error.

Best,

issue_title: Default timeout should be None and block indefinitely
issue_body:
Defaulting the [fido.fetch(...) timeout](https://github.com/Yelp/fido/blob/master/fido/fido.py#L143) value to 1 second goes againt the [principle of least surpise](http://c2.com/cgi/wiki?PrincipleOfLeastSurprise).

The default should be `None` and the behavior should block indefinitely.

Others libs that follow this pattern for default timeouts:
- [Requests](https://github.com/kennethreitz/requests/blob/587eb193ddbc2dfc10c2bdbf1718cd2267a0e77f/requests/sessions.py#L393)
- [concurrent.futures](https://docs.python.org/3/library/concurrent.futures.html#concurrent.futures.Future.result)
- [python socket stdlib](https://docs.python.org/2/library/socket.html#socket.getdefaulttimeout)

This breaks backwards compatibility in behavior so this should be a major version bump with the appropriate documentation.

issue_title: linkify doesn't detect URLs which use new top level domains
issue_body: ``` >>> import bleach >>> bleach.linkify('http://oskibearsin.com/') u'http://oskibearsin.com/' ``` ... great ... ``` >>> bleach.linkify('http://oskibearsin.space/') u'http://oskibearsin.space/' ``` ... not good, since this is a totally valid domain name! I'm actually a little surprised that linkify won't accept any domain in an otherwise well-formed HTTP URL; this will fail to linkify unqualified intranet URLs: ``` >>> bleach.linkify('http://myintranet/') u'http://myintranet/' ``` /cc @jschwab

issue_body:
Hi, I'm having some issues using Boto3 to connect to AWS services. I have some code in a program of mine that looks roughly like this.

```
boto3.set_stream_logger(name='botocore')
sqs = boto3.client('sqs',aws_access_key_id=aws_access_key,aws_secret_access_key=aws_secret_key)

I am using boto3/botocore in AWS Lambda to perform some management tasks against the AWS API. I was noticing that some of my lambda functions would timeout when I started hammering the API and was getting throttled. It appeared that Boto3 was using it's exponential backoff logic as intended. What I am trying to figure out is if there is any kind of timeout logic to the exponential backoff logic. It appears that there is a maxRetries option available, but other than that is Boto3/botocore's default behavior to keep retrying the API with exponential backoff until AWS returns a 503/RequestLimitExceeded error or succeeds?

I am trying to figure out a good balance for my Lambda timeout time between letting Boto retry and not having my lambda run for 5 minutes.

Hi,

I'm getting the above error when trying to use Route53 service. I have investigated and concluded that the fault is [the copying of functions from AWSHTTPConnection to AWSHTTPSConnection](https://github.com/boto/botocore/blob/37bbdc3ffc40e43500d8e044b418947dce4f7482/botocore/awsrequest.py#L256-L261):

```python
# Now we need to set the methods we overrode from AWSHTTPConnection
# onto AWSHTTPSConnection.  This is just a shortcut to avoid
# copy/pasting the same code into AWSHTTPSConnection.
for name, function in AWSHTTPConnection.__dict__.items():
    if inspect.isfunction(function):
        setattr(AWSHTTPSConnection, name, function)
```

The problem is that this ends up overriding the `__init__` function too, so the `VerifiedHTTPSConnection` constructor is never invoked. I don't know why this worked in the past, or why it has become a problem recently. I have locally patched that file to add `and function.__name__ != '__init__'` to the `if` above, and copied manually the constructor to `AWSHTTPSConnection` (to invoke `VerifiedHTTPSConnection` constructor). Things seem to work fine now.

issue_title: MagicBot StateMachine breaks when its last state is timed and has a default state
issue_body:
In the example below I would expect after b is done the state machine would transition to either the **default** or **a** state. Instead the console shows the state machine switching back and forth between the **default** and **a** state (it never transitions from **a** to **b**). If you run this code in the simulator with teleop enabled the console shows this:

> 19:36:23:486 WARNING : robot               : Default MagicRobot.disabledPeriodic() method... Overload me!
19:36:23:642 INFO    : nt                  : CONNECTED 127.0.0.1 port 51670 (pynetworktables 2017.0.8)
19:36:23:685 WARNING : pyfrc.sim.ui        : pygame not detected, real joystick support not loaded
a
b
default
a
default
a
default
a
default
a
default

Calling the StateMachine done method seems to fix this issue, maybe it should be called automatically at the end of the final state?

Here's the code:

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3

import wpilib
from magicbot import StateMachine, state, timed_state, MagicRobot
from magicbot.state_machine import default_state

class SM(StateMachine):

    @default_state
    def d(self):
        print('default')

    @state(first=True)
    def a(self):
        print('a')
        self.next_state('b')

    @timed_state(duration=.01)
    def b(self):
        print('b')


class MyRobot(MagicRobot):

    sm = SM

    def createObjects(self):
        pass

    def teleopPeriodic(self):
        self.sm.engage()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    wpilib.run(MyRobot)


```

issue_title: Why is `gaussian` so opinionated about the image value range
issue_body:
## Description

Trying to apply `skimage.filters.gaussian` to a floating image may result in a `ValueError` if any value is outside [0, 1]. For `gaussian` it shouldn't matter what values are in the image.

```pytb
In [1]: import numpy as np
In [2]: from skimage.filters import gaussian
In [3]: img = np.array([[10.0, -10.0], [-4, 3]], dtype=np.float32)
In [4]: gaussian(img, 1)
---------------------------------------------------------------------------
ValueError                                Traceback (most recent call last)
<ipython-input-15-b8596e79c5dc> in <module>()
----> 1 gaussian(img, 1)

/home/oli/Projects/pbl2/env/lib/python3.5/site-packages/skimage/filters/_gaussian.py in gaussian(image, sigma, output, mode, cval, multichannel)
    102         if len(sigma) != image.ndim:
    103             sigma = np.concatenate((np.asarray(sigma), [0]))
--> 104     image = img_as_float(image)
    105     return ndi.gaussian_filter(image, sigma, mode=mode, cval=cval)

/home/oli/Projects/pbl2/env/lib/python3.5/site-packages/skimage/util/dtype.py in img_as_float(image, force_copy)
    299
    300     """
--> 301     return convert(image, np.float64, force_copy)
    302
    303

/home/oli/Projects/pbl2/env/lib/python3.5/site-packages/skimage/util/dtype.py in convert(image, dtype, force_copy, uniform)
    203     if kind_in == 'f':
    204         if np.min(image) < -1.0 or np.max(image) > 1.0:
--> 205             raise ValueError("Images of type float must be between -1 and 1.")
    206         if kind == 'f':
    207             # floating point -> floating point

ValueError: Images of type float must be between -1 and 1.
```

## Way to reproduce

Simply run the code snippet:

```python
import numpy as np
from skimage.filters import gaussian
img = np.array([[10.0, -10.0], [-4, 3]], dtype=np.float32)
gaussian(img, 1)
```

This issue is to add the following getter's to the Python SpatialPooler:

getOverlapScores
getBoostedOverlapScores

This would be implemented by adding self._overlaps and self._boostedOverlap member variables.  These should be initialized in the constructor to all zeros.

This will be a backward incompatible change, in that previously serialized spatial poolers will not load once this change has been made. As such, we should increment the VERSION flag in spatial_pooler.py and then in __setstate__() we should add the members in for older versions of the SP.

Tests should be added to ensure we are getting the correct results for overlap and boostedOverlap.

A corresponding change needs to be made to the C++ implementation.

Grok/Zope is creating a cookie for every user, even the anonymous user. 
That is a problem.  Mostly I have anonymous users browsing.  I would like to serve them from the NGINX cache.  But if there is a cookie, then NGINX does not cache, it passes it through to my servers, and that overloads the ZOPE servers. 

One simple option is to manage this in the NGINX server.  For every anonymous user create an X-Anonymous header, and then NGINX will delete the cookie.  But that is still creating a new session for every request that reaches the ZOPE servers.  Not good. 

There is the option to only create cookies on the Post requests, but I fear that will lead to throwing an error.  Because Zope.publisher calls   call zope.app.publisher  _maybePlacefullyAuthenticate(request, ob) 

This zope.publisher is the one piece of Zope I do not know well.   It is complicated.   The Grok version is even more complicated. I worry that if I wade in, it will take a long time to figure out what to do. 


Any advice?

issue_title: BIND not working as expected in SPARQL
issue_body:
Ok, consider the following data:

``` turtle
@prefix s: <http://schema.org/> .
@prefix x: <http://example.org/> .

x:john a s:Person ; s:name "John" .
x:jane a s:Person ; s:name "Jane" ; s:knows x:john.
x:ecorp a s:Organization ; s:name "Evil Corp" ; s:employee x:jane . 
```

and the following query:

``` sparql
PREFIX s: <http://schema.org/>
PREFIX x: <http://example.org/>

#CONSTRUCT { ?s ?p ?o }
SELECT ?s ?p ?o ?x # for debugging
WHERE {
  ?x a s:Person .
  { ?x ?p ?o . BIND (?x as ?s) } UNION
  { ?s ?p ?x . BIND (?x as ?o) }
}
```

The goal of the query is to extract all incoming and outgoing arcs of every `s:Person` in the data
(and ultimately to construct a graph with those, but I use `SELECT` for debugging).

As I understand, the result should be

| ?s | ?p | ?o | ?x |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| x:john | rdf:type | s:Person | x:john |
| x:john | s:name | "John" | x:john |
| x:jane | s:knows | x:john | x:john |
| x:jane | rdf:type | s:Person | x:jane |
| x:jane | s:name | "Jane" | x:jane |
| x:jane | s:knows | x:john | x:jane |
| x:ecorp | s:employee | x:jane | x:jane |

At least, this is what I get with Corese and Virtuoso.

But instead I get

| ?s | ?p | ?o | ?x |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | rdf:type | s:Person | x:john |
|  | s:name | "John" | x:john |
| x:jane | s:knows |  | x:john |
|  | rdf:type | s:Person | x:jane |
|  | s:name | "Jane" | x:jane |
|  | s:knows | x:john | x:jane |
| x:ecorp | s:employee |  | x:jane |

so the `BIND` (in this configuration) does not seem to work.

NB: the `UNION` is not the cause of the problem. I have the same problem without any `UNION`.

issue_title: Let objects change their gsparams after construction
issue_body:
Something that comes up from time to time is people specifying gsparams in a Convolve object and expecting that to affect the component objects.  

This wasn't really possible to make happen in v1.6 (at least not easily), but I think given some of the changes in our noboost branch (specifically that construction of _sbp is delayed until it is needed), it would be possible to do now.

Therefore, I'd like to make the following changes to how this works for v2.0:

1. Add a `withGSParams(gsparams)` method to all GSObjects, which would return a new object with the gsparams attribute changed to the given value.
2. Change classes that contain other objects (Convolution, Transformation, Sum, etc.) to apply any specified gsparams option to the contained component(s).
3. For the ones that wrap multiple objects, if the given gsparams=None, then find the most restrictive combination of parameters from the components and apply that to all the components.

The last two would be API changes, so if people think it's a good idea, v2.0 would be a good time to do it.

Pinging @jchiang87 who was recently bit by this "feature" in v1.6.
Also @sweverett who requested this feature for Balrog.

issue_title: Add support for multiple Etcd endpoints
issue_body:
Looking at the docs specified here, it appears we can only specify a single Etcd endpoint.
https://patroni.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ENVIRONMENT.html#environment

The limitation here is that if that specific endpoint is not available on start-up, it's obviously not going to be able to auto-discover the other members to establish a connection.  Seems we could avoid this issue by allowing users to manually specify each member endpoint or each proxy endpoint they have available.  Patroni could then cycle through the endpoints until it is able to establish a connection.

issue_title: verify_oauth uses url params instead of Authorization headers for token info
issue_body:
### Description

``verify_oauth`` uses url params instead of Authorization headers for token info.
Using Authorization header is the recommended way for verifying tokens.

Using url params could lead to leaking access tokens to external services. We faced this issue during testing OpenTracing. Authorization headers provides a more structured way in preventing leaking access tokens (e.g. in opentracing tags).

### Expected behaviour

Using Authorization headers for verifying tokens.

### Actual behaviour

Using URL params for verifying tokens.

### Steps to reproduce


### Additional info:

Output of the commands:

- `python --version`
Python 3.5.2
- `pip show connexion | grep "^Version\:"`

issue_title: [OAuth] Option to make a scopes optionnal but at least one of them is ok
issue_body:
Hello There,

Just a little question regarding this issue: https://github.com/zalando/connexion/issues/256
Which was fixed here: https://github.com/zalando/connexion/pull/257

I was actually looking for the actually patched functionnality, actually it is not possible to make a validation for at least one scope (exclusive or), let me explain better.

What I actually have:
- 3 scopes: read, write, delete
- one route can have multiple scopes, but I don't want my user to have all of them at once

So, I tried to put multiple scopes in one path, for a specific route, like this:

```yaml
securityDefinitions:
  oauth-implicit:
    ...
    scopes:
      read: "read"
      write: "write"
...
paths:
  /testing:
    get:
      security:
        oauth-implicit-read:
          - read
          - write
```

The problem here is that now, to access this endpoint, you need to have the scopes read and write together. Since I want my user to have just one of them to access it, I had the choice to make a new route with separated scopes, which is making me duplicates my endpoints, which is not really what rest is for, since it will duplicate access to my ressources.

So, the solution I came with, even if it's not really good, was to make multiple securityDefinition with one scope for each (I know this is not beautiful, but hey, I'm do what I can xD ):

```yaml

securityDefinitions:
  oauth-implicit-read:
    ...
    scopes:
      read: "read"
  oauth-implicit-write:
    ...
    scopes:
      write: "write"
...
paths:
  /testing:
    get:
      security:
        oauth-implicit-read:
          - read
        oauth-implicit-write:
          - write
```

The problem here is that now, you can access my route without a scope with this, my two scopes are optionnals now, if you have a bad scope you will have a 403 error.

I think this is actually a bug for this part, but I also think that it can be better to have the possibility to make an exclusive or somehow while putting our security in our endpoint definition.

Is there a possibility to make the older solution working again with a specific tag in our securityDefinition (I think it would be better if we can put it in the security tag in the endpoint definition to have better control for it but if you got a better solution in mind I'm ok too ;) )?

Thanks for reading, and thanks for your work, this is greatly appreciated :+1: 

Regards,
Diliz

PS: I'm looking to what is makable and will make a pull request if I come up with a good solution, this is not a breaking change since this will be an option to set if we want to have this usage of scopes.

issue_title: Add GSSError, inheriting from KrbError, and use it everywhere KrbError is currently used.
issue_body: See https://github.com/requests/requests-kerberos/pull/75

Relations are defined on a collection. Relation permissions should be based on the permissions of the corresponding collection. Implement the following permissions rules:

* Query relations: VIEW permission on the collection
* Create, edit, delete relations: EDIT permission on the collection
* Relations are SHARED when a corresponding collection is shared. 
* Relation OWNER is the owner of the corresponding collection. 
* Relations are deleted when a corresponding collection is deleted.

issue_title: DyNet no longer searches for open GPU

```
SchemaField.from_api_repr({'name': 'colname', 'type': 'INT64'})
```

I believe this is due to the line

```
mode=api_repr['mode'].upper(),
```

which does not properly handle when the `mode` property is missing.

Related to https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/issues/3723 as it is often convenient to omit the mode parameter when defining a schema file by hand.

issue_title: PythonInterpreter fails to discover OSX Python binaries
issue_body:
The real path of OSX python binaries can look like so:
`/System/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/2.7/Resources/Python.app/Contents/MacOS/Python`

The binary discovery logic in PythonInterpreter misses binaries like these due to case-sensitive regexes here: https://github.com/pantsbuild/pex/blob/master/pex/interpreter.py#L181

This was discovered in https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/issues/1908

issue_title: improve missing-deps-suggest suggestion sorting when there are multiple matching jar_libraries
issue_body:
Jar libraries are special because they may have multiple classpath entries associated with them that overlap. Ideally, missing-deps-suggest would prefer jar_library targets where the jar containing the missing type is one of the declared jars. 

Currently, any jar_library that has a transitive dependency will show up. If the missing type is a common one, that can be frustrating because it makes the suggestions less useful.

At a minimum, I think that jar libraries should be sorted by whether the matching jars are declared by the target. But I think it'd be better still to just elide the other jar libraries if one with a declared jar is found.

issue_title: Pants pypi package and its python package namespace don't match.
issue_body: The python package namespace is `pants` but the pypi package is `pantsbuild.pants`.  This allows for both the [pypi `pants` package](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/pants) and `pantsbuild.pants` to be resolved to the same PYTHONPATH and then lead to mysterious import errors.  Pants should be a good citizen and have its code package structure match its pypi artifact name or at least the pantsbuild prefix of that name to avoid this sort of conflict.

issue_title: Wrong custom service class definition when the name is sorted before 'MXNetVisionService'
issue_body:
https://github.com/awslabs/mxnet-model-server/blob/f98a2763f71695a59a69974c367c6f8e43203373/mms/mxnet_model_server.py#L150

The assumption that "# The overrided class is the last one in class_defs" is wrong because 'class_defs' is a list returned by inspect.getmembers(), which returns a list of member classes names sorted by the name. 
https://docs.python.org/2/library/inspect.html#inspect.getmembers

So, using "mode_class_name = class_defs[-1].__name__" leads to wrong class name when the class name string is something smaller than the base class name.

Thanks.

issue_body:
## Summary

Right now, looks like we need to clone both a Kolibri repository and the specific plugin we need to build, and then run the devserver to have it serve the assets. It's hard on a devops and CI perspective to start building the plugins:

- Which version of Kolibri do I need to clone to get a plugin building? We'll like to pin a plugin to a version of Kolibri in the long term.
- I'm basically just replicating the Kolibri build pipeline, but with one small change. This consumes both devops and computing resources.
- What specific steps should I take to get a plugin building and releasable? Right now, the plugin build process is very dev-focused. Hence I'm having a hard time to figure out what steps I need to do on a continuous integration perspective. `yarn install && yarn run build`, `pip install -e && make assets` all are no dice. Running `make dist` inside the plugin itself doesn't work too.

## Possible solutions

The goal is to have `make dist` produce a working `whl` file that's ready to be uploaded to PyPI.

1. When `make dist` is run, clone Kolibri on Git based on a certain tag, and then run `yarn run build` or some other magic using the cloned Kolibri dir. `make clean` should remove said directory.
1. Similar to the one above, but only fetch the exact files that are needed to build. So maybe only the vue files and the frontend build dependencies?
1. Make the plugin fully standalone (has all the dependencies needed to build its assets). Not sure what this would involve, but is the cleanest solution.

The reason seems to lie in `/docker/api/container.py`, line 208-210
```python
def port(self, container, private_port):

...

h_ports = port_settings.get(s_port + '/udp')

Instead of basically only accepting a port number for `private_port` and trying for udp and tcp exposed ports, I'd suggest that the method rather accepts an explicit `private_port` input like `1234/tcp`.

A composite layer that rescales the Hamiltonian would add a lot of convenience.  It would rescale the input Hamiltonian to fit within specified ranges, then reverse the rescaling so the energies match the original Hamiltonian.

I currently use the following function to rescale Hamiltonians, with an additional feature that allows clipping large values so that the Hamiltonian doesn't have to be scaled down as much (the tradeoff being that the Hamiltonian is not a faithful rescaling if clipping < 1).

```
def rescale(hh, jj, h_range, j_range, clipping=1.):
    """Rescale the Hamiltonian.

    Clipping truncates values at a certain fraction of the provided range to lessen the
    required compression.
    """

    # Unpack the bounds.
    min_h, max_h = map(float, h_range)
    min_j, max_j = map(float, j_range)

    # Find the natural rescaling factor
    scalars = [
        1.,
        max([h / min_h for h in hh]),
        max([h / max_h for h in hh]),
        max([j / min_j for j in jj.values()]),
        max([j / max_j for j in jj.values()]),
        ]

    scalar = max(scalars)
    # Adjust for clipping
    scalar *= clipping

    hh_scaled = [min_h if h/scalar <= min_h else max_h if h/scalar >= max_h else h/scalar for h in hh]
    jj_scaled = {k: min_j if v/scalar <= min_j else max_j if v/scalar >= max_j else v/scalar for k, v in jj.iteritems()}

    return hh_scaled, jj_scaled, scalar

```

issue_title: Update Installing SSL on a Load Balancer Doc
issue_body:
> To enable SSL, you will need to open port 443 on the load balancer and forward it to port 80 on the routers

http://docs.deis.io/en/latest/managing_deis/ssl-endpoints/#installing-ssl-on-a-load-balancer

Starting deis 1.6 we enabled proxy protocol. If proxy protocol is enabled, the load balancer (at least for ELBs) has to use TCP 443:443.

If you use instance protocol 443, you need to set the ssl termination on the router.

Do we recommend disabling proxy protocol? In that case they can still use 443:80 as mentioned in the docs.

issue_title: Conda on windows can reliably host long running applications
issue_body:
When running on windows conda can not reliably host server applications. Windows locks executables and dlls that are in use, even ones that are hard linked. This means that when a long running process is running - even in a clean environment - the python dll in the root environment can not be removed.

issue_title: Outdated conda notification does not respect `-q` arg
issue_body: The notify_outdated_conda warning checks only `if context.notify_outdated_conda`, but I think it should check `if context.notify_outdated_conda and not context.quiet`. If I specify the `-q` flag, I don't want to see this warning.

@stefanseefeld asked me to submit this issue.  We have discussed this before at length.  Conda-build needs to completely rebuild its index after any package is built, so that that package is then installable for the test phase.  It would be a large optimization to conda-build if conda were able to modify the existing index to add the new package, rather than re-generate the entire index.

Some more info (possibly outdated) here:

This happens in two parts.  The first adds the package to the on-disk repodata.json file: https://github.com/conda/conda-build/blob/master/conda_build/build.py#L755
MichaelS
The second re-reads the on-disk repodata.json into the memory object: https://github.com/conda/conda-build/blob/master/conda_build/build.py#L1239
MichaelS
It would be ideal to replace the second part with a small modification to the in-memory index during the first part.

issue_title: Conda 4.3 incorrectly adds `defaults` with `--channel` CLI flag usage
issue_body:
From way back at the beginning of conda 4.3 releases, we have

https://github.com/conda/conda/blob/d7683df409ae88a1ec8fc6468dedd0acb45a0e67/conda/base/context.py#L448-L456

The intention was:

> If no configuration information is given in condarc configuration files or in environment variables, and if a user specifies at least one `--channel` command line flag, add `defaults` to the channel list if it's not already included.

Unfortunately, the logic was wrong.  The line

    if argparse_channels and argparse_channels == self._channels: 

should have been

    if argparse_channels and argparse_channels != self._channels: 

So we ended up with the opposite behavior.

The solution here, I believe, is to just get rid of this special case behavior altogether now, since we've long ago broken the original behavior of the `-c` implementation.

issue_title: Conda install homemade package leave Pip listing in 'conda list'
issue_body:
I have build a conda package from a git repo, referring to (http://conda.pydata.org/docs/build_tutorials/pkgs2.html) . 

The code is public:
https://tlelson@bitbucket.org/tlelson/tims_py_utils.git

It is build it with 'conda build' and uploaded to anaconda.  Installing into a new conda environment with:

``` bash
conda install -c https://conda.anaconda.org/tle tims_py_utils
```

Doing a 'conda list' returns 2 entries! 

``` bash
...
tims-py-utils-2.1-12      g81423b5                  <pip>
tims_py_utils             2.1                 12_g81423b5    file:///Users/tims-MBP/miniconda3/conda-bld/osx-64/tims_py_utils-2.1-12_g81423b5.tar.bz2
...
```

Why is the package listed like it has been installed separately with pip? '--no-pip' option does not display the listing but my understanding was that this option removes "pip-only" listings which this is not.

``` bash
$ conda list -h
...
  --no-pip              Do not include pip-only installed packages.
```

**Actual Questions**
Should I be doing something different in the build.sh to avoid the package going to 'site-packages'?
Why does the package list as being installed from file not the anaconda channel?
What am I doing differently to other conda packages that install without the pip item?

When installing anaconda-verify, there's no apparent reason to touch nltk or install libgfortran.

```
u0018446@sys-86341:~/anaconda$ conda install anaconda-verify

issue_title: Reindex of an empty list using REINDEX_SELECTION causes massive reindex
issue_body:
### Expected Behavior
In a reindex action, If there is no index in the list after the filtering stage, curator should raise an exception and don't send the task to elasticsearch. In addition it should give the option to ignore that exception using `ignore_empty_list: true` but the reindex shouldn't be executed 

### Actual Behavior
Elasticsearch reindex api seems to accept an empty list of indexes as source and it takes it like a `*` (all the indexes)
The reindex task is sent to elasticsearch an it tries to run the reindex using all the indexes as source. Hopefully, the task will fail with a validation error but I think it's really dangerous to depend only on Elasticsearch's validation

### Steps to Reproduce the Problem
1 - Create an action like:

```
  1:
    description: "Reindex last day's index into monthly"
    action: reindex
    options:
      ignore_empty_list: true
      requests_per_second: 5000
      wait_interval: 30
      max_wait:      -1
      request_body:
        source:
          index: REINDEX_SELECTION
        dest:
          index: monthly-2018.01
    filters:
      - filtertype: pattern
        kind: prefix
        value: asdasd-
```

2 - Ensure that the filter doesn't match any index

3 - Debug output of curator on action execution:
```
...
2018-01-31 21:26:49,309 DEBUG              curator.utils        iterate_filters:1087 Post-instance: []
2018-01-31 21:26:49,309 DEBUG     curator.actions.reindex               __init__:1055 REQUEST_BODY = {'dest': {'index': 'monthly-2018.01'}, 'source': {'index': 'REINDEX_SELECTION'}}
2018-01-31 21:26:49,309 DEBUG     curator.actions.reindex               __init__:1228 Reindexing indices: []
2018-01-31 21:26:49,309 DEBUG                curator.cli         process_action:99   Doing the action here.
2018-01-31 21:26:49,309 INFO      curator.actions.reindex              do_action:1335 Commencing reindex operation
2018-01-31 21:26:49,309 DEBUG     curator.actions.reindex              do_action:1337 REINDEX: request body: {'dest': {'index': 'monthly-2018.01'}, 'source': {'index': []}} with arguments: refresh=True requests_per_second=5000 slices=1 timeout=60s wait_for_active_shards=1 wait_for_completion=True
2018-01-31 21:26:49,666 ERROR                curator.cli                    run:184  Failed to complete action: reindex.  <class 'curator.exceptions.FailedExecution'>: Exception encountered.  Rerun with loglevel DEBUG and/or check Elasticsearch logs for more information. Exception: TransportError(400, u'action_request_validation_exception', u'Validation Failed: 1: use _all if you really want to copy from all existing indexes;')
```

### Specifications

  - Version: Curator 5.4.1
  - Platform: Docker container with base image `python:2.7-alpine`
  - Elasticsearch version `5.5.0`

## Detailed Description
If you have a reindex action using `REINDEX_SELECTION` as source index, and a couple of filters on that action filtering out every index (i.e returning an empty list), then elasticsearch is instructed to perform a reindex from `[]` to `dest-index`.

This doesn't seem the expected behavior on using filters. If no index match the filters, no task should executed

issue_title: az acs list should list all acs instances with resource group as an optional value
issue_body:
In most cases customers will place ACS instances in its own resource group as each acs instance creates a lot of resources. 
Yes, they can put multiples in one rg, However, having to add yet another parameter to get the norm feels overly complex. I can't speak for VM, vmss but I'd suggest we go the opposite standard. If I call az VM list, the default behavior is to list them all, and if I want a specific rg I pass it in as rg is a standard parameter. 

From @rgardler 
+1

I do not want acs commands to have to conform to a model designed for VMs. ACS is about managing containers, not VMS. Our users should not need to worry about the underlying Azure concepts such as resource group, VMSS, VM, NSG, LB etc. etc. Our job is to abstract away from these as much as possible.

Today ACS is only an IaaS service. We will always see customers using it as IaaS and enjoying the flexibility this brings, but at the same time we will be moving more and more towards a managed service and we should, wherever possible, define the CLI tools to account for this.

issue_title: az network watcher connection-monitor query (need to add option to query list of connection monitors)
issue_body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Before now we had cmdlet which allow to query snapshot of specified connection monitor:
az network watcher connection-monitor query --location --name

We added another API which allow to query list or all connection monitors in the region.
Here is rest API spec: https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/blob/master/specification/network/resource-manager/Microsoft.Network/stable/2018-06-01/networkWatcher.json (line 1848).

Example of usage is here: https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/blob/master/specification/network/resource-manager/Microsoft.Network/stable/2018-06-01/examples/NetworkWatcherQueryConnectionMonitors.json

**Describe the solution you'd like**
we would like to have an option for customer to specify 1 connection monitor, list connection monitors or nothing. In last 2 cases, new API will be used.


**Describe alternatives you've considered**
We could create a new cmdlet, but I doubt it's an optimal solution...

**Additional context**
Add any other context or screenshots about the feature request here.

issue_title: retry of uploading of a chunk with a SAS token adds token a second time
issue_body: Hi, we are uploading files with a size of ~4GB with multiple connections: blob_service = BlockBlobService(account_name=, sas_token=) blob_service.create_blob_from_path(container, file_name, path, max_connections=10) During this it happens from time to time that HTTP requests are failing and then retried. But also all retries are failing as well resulting in a failing create_blob_from_path with such an error: AuthenticationFailedServer failed to authenticate the request. Make sure the value of Authorization header is formed correctly including the signature.\\nRequestId:64598a84-0001-0139-2864-e0c69d000000\\nTime:2017-06-08T14:35:08.7026769Zsr is mandatory. Cannot be empty With help of the Azure support and then some debugging in https://github.com/Azure/azure-storage-python/blob/master/azure/storage/_http/httpclient.py#L105 we found that the SAS token is appended a second time to the request url. This makes the SAS token invalid and results in the authentication error. I could identify where this happens but if this can be located the fix might be simple.

issue_title: Provide consistent behavior for 429 status code
issue_body:
If service sends 429 back, then we must look for retry-after header. If retry-after header is not available, then by default retry after 20 seconds. If retry-after header is available, then we must wait until the time provided by the service before retrying again. Make this a pluggable component so that if customers intend to add their own behavior for 429, they can.

From https://github.com/Azure/autorest/issues/2601

Looks like the problem (https://github.com/requests/requests-kerberos/issues/64) is not going to be resolved anytime soon. As discuss, we need to provide the client a customized way to handle redirects

issue_title: ClientSession inside an aiohttp Server does not support connection pooling
issue_body:
I've got a aiohttp server that makes multiple http requests to other services in response to its inbound requests, I want to use a ClientSession for each inbound request to handle any cookie state that may be needed across the downstream requests it makes - but this means my server makes no use of connection-pooling/keep-alive when making downstream requests.

I could share a single ClientSession across all inbound requests, but then cookie/header state within the ClientSession would be re-used across inbound requests, which would not be correct. I could perhaps create a SharedClientSession object that handled switching between cookies/headers with a single underlying ClientSession, but that seems like I'm fighting the system.

Is there a supported way to do this? The most elegant way I can imagine would be a single ClientSession per inbound request with a global tcpconnector - except the ClientSession takes ownership and closes it, making it impossible to use in this way.

issue_title: Add more specific metadata to request headers
issue_body:
For the purposes of tracking usage adoption, it would be nice to include some library and version information in the HTTP requests. 

I believe the `requests` library under the hood of `searchtweets` sends something similar to `'User-Agent': 'python-requests/1.2.0'` in its requests. Perhaps we could modify the headers like (roughly): 
```python
version = searchtweets.__version__
useragent = 'search-tweets-python/{}'.format(version)
headers = {'User-Agent': useragent}
```
I'm not sure if it would be best set in the [`request()` method](https://github.com/twitterdev/search-tweets-python/blob/master/searchtweets/result_stream.py#L120) or in [the `make_session()` method](https://github.com/twitterdev/search-tweets-python/blob/master/searchtweets/result_stream.py#L30). The `requests` docs mention doing it in both places ([former](http://docs.python-requests.org/en/latest/user/quickstart/#custom-headers), [latter](http://docs.python-requests.org/en/latest/user/advanced/#prepared-requests)).

@binaryaaron @fionapigott @jeffakolb are there other metadata things that would be useful in the headers?

I'm trying to get yara-python 3.7.0 working with the cuckoo module.

I followed the instructions to clone the repo recursively, run `python setup.py build --enable-cuckoo` and `python setup.py install`.

Now I'm trying to get some basic tests running.

I'm setting up a string with a simple condition, calling `yara.compile(source=foo)`, setting up a dict with my cuckoo report, and then calling `rule.match(data='MALWARE', module_data=modules_dict)`

yr_rules_scan_mem returns error code 6. That part shouldn't matter much.

Line 1517 calls `handle_error(error, filepath)`

That takes us down to the call to PyErr_Format on line 934, which causes a segfault because the parameter `extra` is NULL.

I'm not sure what the proper fix would be here. Switching to `handle_error(error, filepath || data)` seems like the quickest/easiest.

issue_title: Model.validate() raises DataError in face, instead of ValidationError as said in documentation
issue_body:
The [documentation here](http://schematics.readthedocs.io/en/latest/usage/validation.html#validation-errors):

However I got `DataError` exception when required-constraint is not satisfied.

```python
ipdb> exp
DataError({'password': ConversionError([ErrorMessage('This field is required.', None)])})
ipdb> exp.messages
{'password': ConversionError([ErrorMessage('This field is required.', None)])}
```

And `DataError.messages` is not json-serializable like `ValidationError.messages`.

issue_title: URLType regex doesn't match urls that have subdomains with underscores
issue_body:
If you use the URL_REGEX value from the URLType, it doesn't match the following valid url: `http://my_subdomain.mydomain.com/` If you remove the underscore, it works. 

It seems like the best thing here might be to use another project's url regex. Django, for example, has one that's well-tested and does work on the above url: https://github.com/django/django/blob/f8cc464452f495fce2a3d6f7494396c8f798a1e6/django/core/validators.py#L67

I don't mean "memory leak" in terms of unreachable memory after the Python process quits, I mean memory that is being allocated in the backwards pass, when it should be being freed. I mentioned this problem in #199 , but I think it should be opened as an issue.

For a simple function
```
import autograd.numpy as np
from autograd import grad

def F(x,z):
    for i in range(100):
        z = np.dot(x,z)
    return np.sum(z)
dF = grad(F)
```

and a procedure to measure memory usage
```
from memory_profiler import memory_usage
def make_data():
    np.random.seed(0)
    D = 1000
    x = np.random.randn(D,D)
    x = np.dot(x,x.T)
    z = np.random.randn(D,D)
    return x,z

def m():
    from time import sleep
    x,z = make_data()
    gx = dF(x,z)
    sleep(0.1)
    return gx

mem_usage = np.array(memory_usage(m,interval=0.01))
mem_usage -= mem_usage[0]
```

and a manual gradient of the same function

```
def grad_dot_A(g,A,B):
    ga = np.dot(g,B.T)
    ga = np.reshape(ga,np.shape(A))
    return ga

def grad_dot_B(g,A,B):
    gb = np.dot(A.T,g)
    gb = np.reshape(gb, np.shape(B))
    return gb

def dF(x, z):
    z_stack = []
    for i in list(range(100)):
        z_stack.append(z)
        z = np.dot(x, z)
    retval = np.sum(z)

    # Begin Backward Pass
    g_retval = 1
    g_x = 0

    # Reverse of: retval = np.sum(z)
    g_z = repeat_to_match_shape(g_retval, z)
    for i in reversed(list(range(100))):

        # Reverse of: z = np.dot(x, z)
        z = z_stack.pop()
        tmp_g0 = grad_dot_A(g_z, x, z)
        tmp_g1 = grad_dot_B(g_z, x, z)
        g_z = 0
        g_x += tmp_g0
        g_z += tmp_g1
    return g_x
```

I get the following memory usage profile:

![image](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/161935/25681799/9fed2d90-3023-11e7-963d-ea413baf8797.png)

If I replace the dot gradient with the ones used in the manual code, I get the same memory profile, nothing improves. 

If I replace the dot product with element-wise multiply, I get a different memory profile, but still not what I would expect:

![image](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/161935/25681971/5b6f35d6-3024-11e7-96ea-3fac8289e95b.png)

I would love to help figure this out, but I'm not sure where to start. First thing is of course to document the problem.

issue_title: The search subsystem also needs a fast sync option
issue_body:
After working on the search subsystem, I'm realizing that the developer UX can be improved significantly if we build a "fast sync" option for search as well. 

Similar to the [blockstack fast sync](https://github.com/blockstack/blockstack-core/issues/272) it can fetch an index (signed by developers) and immediately start serving search results in a new deployment. We can start a background process that creates a (trusted) index locally. 

Until the local index is created, yes, you're trusting the developers for the data you got but it's exactly the same security model as the general fast sync of Blockstack Core and can improve the UX a lot.

Keeping the discussion flag on this until I get more feedback and we get a yes/no decision for implementing this feature.

Not assigning a milestone right now.
Blockstack-core are not working with Gaia storage driver.

issue_title: Change how we package runs with no RunStop document into a Header.
issue_body: Currently, if a run has no run stop (which means something went *seriously* wrong during a scan) we set `header['stop'] = None`. This leads to some annoying checks throughout the codebase. Simply leaving off the `'stop'` key such that `header['stop']` raises a `KeyError` would actually be simpler.

issue_body: Just a reminder that the current defaults are long out of date.  Need the latest and greatest before running any large tests.

Attempting to parse a function that uses Python 3's annotations raises a `ParsingError`.
Here's a minimal example:

```python
import inspect
from redbaron import RedBaron

def f(x: int) -> int:
    pass

red = RedBaron(inspect.getsource(f.__code__))
```

A `ParsingError` is raised for both attributes and return annotations.

I would like to use mesa for a project I am working on, but I am not sure how best to approach it. It will likely lead to new contributions to the base code, or at least an extension.

The idea is to have adult agents and child agents, add network connections between agents, children learn from adults, adults "die", children mature into adults, and then a new generation of child agents is created, and the process iterates again. At first, I thought that this would work best as a new kind of Scheduler, because it manages which agents are activated when. However, it also seems to be a special case of a BatchRunner, but where each new iteration is dependent on the previous iteration's output.

As for data collection in my case, the most important metrics would involve the final states of agents after each iteration.

Before I make a first draft, does anyone have any suggestions for the best way to build this?

issue_title: CatalogueImportError will never being raised during catalogue import
issue_body:
So, this code https://github.com/django-oscar/django-oscar/blob/master/src/oscar/management/commands/oscar_import_catalogue.py#L41 will never executed, because `CatalogueImportError` exception class didn't even imported in the `partner.importer` module. Instead of this, `ImportingError` used, but there are no places where it can be caught. 

It would be great to get explanations on this exceptions and how to use it properly.

In Gentoo, Define's are passed by the initscript as -D arguments read in /etc/conf.d/apache

LE seems to ignore that. As a result, since the "Listen 443" directive is inside IfDefine blocks, it is systematically overlooked by LE since it doesn't know about the active Define directives.

LE will therefore add a "Listen 443" temporary directive, which will cause apache to fail with a "could not bind to address 0.0.0.0:443" error. LE in turn will fail with "urn:acme:error:connection" since apache is not running during the challenge.

issue_title: file not found in mSubimage call from mExec
issue_body:
When the number of base files is 1, mExec now calls mSubimage.
However, the input file is incorrectly named with extension .fit,
yet the output from mProjectPP has extension .fits.  The mExec
process fails because the output file mosaic.fits is not produced
by mSubimage which has the wrong input filename.

This behavior was not seen in v3.3 because mAdd was used and
it used input tables generated from mImgtbl.  I suggest that an
easy fix would be to adopt this same protocol for the call the
mSubimage: use mImgtbl to generate a table with the correct
filename and then pass that table to mSubimage.

I have a calendar (generated by Google) with a line like so:

> RRULE:FREQ=MONTHLY;UNTIL=20180819;INTERVAL=12;BYDAY=3MO

If I use `rrulestr` to parse this recurrence with a `dtstart` **including timezone info**, the generator fails, because it tries a comparison with the `_until` field which has no timezone info.

Here's a minimal example of what I mean:

```python
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from dateutil.rrule import *

rule_str = 'FREQ=MONTHLY;UNTIL=20180819;INTERVAL=12;BYDAY=3MO'

# this works (no timezone data)
start = datetime(2017,8,21)
rule = rrulestr(rule_str, dtstart=start)
print(list(rule))

# but this does not
start = datetime(2017,8,21,tzinfo=timezone.utc)
rule = rrulestr(rule_str, dtstart=start)
print(list(rule))
```

Output:

```
[datetime.datetime(2017, 8, 21, 0, 0)]
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "mwe_rrule.py", line 17, in <module>
    print(list(rule))
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.6/site-packages/dateutil/rrule.py", line 861, in _iter
    if until and res > until:
TypeError: can't compare offset-naive and offset-aware datetimes
```

EDIT: I monkey patched rrule.py after line 446: 
https://github.com/dateutil/dateutil/blob/1c8c90338a696c8c146c7eb6cc1bf52016912852/dateutil/rrule.py#L446
by adding
```
        if until:
            until = until.replace(tzinfo=self._tzinfo)
```

If you're interested I could make a PR, but maybe you have a better way in mind.

issue_title: Current median yields incorrect value for even lists
issue_body: The current implementation of median combination method yields incorrect values for even lists. Instead of returning the mean of the central values, it returns the left value of the two.

The Matlab project's sapm function signature is:

``pvl_sapm(Module, Ee, celltemp)``

while ours is

``sapm(module, poa_direct, poa_diffuse, temp_cell, airmass_absolute, aoi)``

The Matlab project has used that signature since at least version 1.1 (the oldest that I have). The Python signature started as

``pvl_sapm(Module,Eb,Ediff,Tcell,AM,AOI)``

in commit e74644b and I changed it to the current signature in 5f9bd7c1. If I'm reading the original commit correctly (specifically line 101), I think the units of the irradiance should be W/m**2 despite the docstring's claim of suns.

So that's the current status and history, and I will leave it to you all to decide how you want to go forward.

If we agree on it, we could either completely rely on [pandas](http://pandas-docs.github.io/pandas-docs-travis/contributing.html) or give some rules, where we use our own conventions (in the [wiki](https://github.com/pvlib/pvlib-python/wiki/Development-information) or in the docs).
Start with just `messages`. Field errors can come later.

issue_title: RFE: Add support for soft/virtual deletes at server level.
issue_body:
There are certain use cases where it is not permissible to "hard delete" anything from the database.  Instead, everything must be "virtual deleted" only.  This functionality would be similar to Kinto.js' virtual delete support (but not exactly because Kinto.js will "hard delete" a virtually deleted item on sync).

This request is to support the use cases mentioned above at the server level in one of the following ways:

1. Separate 'write' permissions into 'update' and 'delete'.  This would break backwards compatibility (unless 'write' were kept on in addition to 'update' and 'delete').

1. Allow an administrator to enable/disable HTTP verbs (e.g. DELETE in this instance).  I don't even know if this is possible, as I'm just starting to learn / use Kinto.

    An alternative to this strategy would be to block verbs at a firewall level, though I would prefer this solution to be within Kinto rather than relying on other pieces of software.

1. Provide "virtual delete" as an option that would change the server's default behavior on delete to mark an item as 'deleted' and, maybe, hide such items by default in queries.  This would require adding an attribute to every item (a status like Kinto.js' `_status` with values such as "active" and "deleted").  This would keep backwards compatibility if the server were to assume that any item that is missing this attribute is in Active status.

    I don't know much about Kinto plugins.  However, if this option can be implemented as a plugin, I would be willing to try and take a crack at it.  However, not really knowing Kinto or Python means that hopefully there's a really good tutorial (or sample plugin) out there that teaches you how to do such things.

The purpose of this post is to hopefully start a discussion on this topic and, eventually, provide such functionality within the Kinto server.

issue_body:
Hi,

I noticed that horizontal and vertical bands may be switched around in wavedec2. Here is some IPython notebook code to reproduce this:

```Python
import numpy as np
import pywt
from scipy.misc import imread, imsave
from skimage.draw import polygon
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
%matplotlib inline

img = np.zeros((256, 256))

poly1 = np.array((
        (50, 50),
        (50, 100),
        (100, 100),
        (100, 50)
    ))

poly2 = np.array((
        (150, 100),
        (100, 150),
        (150, 200),
        (200, 150),
    ))

rr, cc = polygon(poly1[:, 0], poly1[:, 1], img.shape)
img[rr, cc] = 1

rr, cc = polygon(poly2[:, 0], poly2[:, 1], img.shape)
img[rr, cc] = 1

db4 = pywt.Wavelet('db4')
img_w = pywt.wavedec2(img, db4, mode='sym', level=1)

fig1, ((ax1, ax2), (ax3, ax4)) = plt.subplots(2, 2)

ax1.imshow(np.absolute(img_w[0]), cmap=plt.cm.gray)
ax1.axis('off')

ax3.set_title('Vertical detail')

issue_title: Automatically add endpoint for the one configured in kernel_*_credentials
issue_body:
If you set a Livy server in `kernel_scala_credentials` or `kernel_python_credentials`, it works automatically when running the corresponding wrapper kernel. But if you start a normal Python notebook on the same Jupyter instance and run the `%manage_spark` magic, it doesn't take that configuration into account at all; you have to re-enter it before you can create a session.

Is there any good reason not to pre-configure an endpoint entry if the user has already gone to the trouble of setting it up for the wrapper kernel? Of course, they still can specify other endpoints in `%manage_spark` but this seems like a convenient shortcut for 90% of use cases (with very little damage done to the other 10%).

Would a pull request along these lines be accepted, or are you opposed to this idea? Thanks :)

issue_title: Error that results when run without root is unclear
issue_body:
When running `letsencrypt` without root, I get

```
An unexpected error occurred:
OSError: [Errno 13] Permission denied: '/etc/letsencrypt'
Please see the logfile 'letsencrypt.log' for more details.
```

The underlying reason is that, to run without root, I need to specify `--config-dir`, `--work-dir`, and `--logs-dir`. However, the client doesn't give me any suggestion about this or about the possibility of doing anything (even reading help) without root access.

While a sysadmin would probably conclude that the reason for the `OSError` is simply that the client needs root to run, it would be great to have a clearer and more useful error message in this case.

When I run with `--text`, I get these lines as output:

```
Congratulations! You have successfully enabled https://02e645.le-test.hoffman-
andrews.com

You should test your configuration at:
https://www.ssllabs.com/ssltest/analyze.html?d=02e645.le-test.hoffman-
andrews.com
```

These lines are artificially wrapped at 80 characters, even though my terminal is much wider than that. This breaks the links and makes them (a) unclickable and (b) hard to paste into a browser.

I think with `--text`, we shouldn't wrap lines, but leave it to the user's terminal.

issue_title: Should we reissue a certificate if different attributes are requested?
issue_body: This was suggested [here](https://community.letsencrypt.org/t/certbot-how-can-i-renew-if-staging-certs-exist-but-i-want-production-certs/46472/8). I'm potentially OK with this change, but we should consider how/if we want to do this very carefully and what attributes we care about. Doing this complicates both our code and our explanation of how Certbot works. There will be some instances like those discussed in the linked forum post where this will be the behavior that is intuitive for people, but we should make sure this is the case and it won't negatively impact others in the future.

issue_body:
Boulder has merged IDN support recently and is planning to enable it in staging and production in the near future (next few weeks most likely).

Boulder/ACME requires that any IDN be converted to ASCII using the [IDNA2008 `ToASCII` encoding method](http://unicode.org/reports/tr46/#ToASCII) before being sent in a CSR/authorization request etc etc.
